description:
Context:
    You are an incident-responder who has just received a partial disk image that was already mounted for you at

        /home/user/incident_data_source

    That directory contains two sub-directories that hold the artefacts you must preserve:

        /home/user/incident_data_source/images
        /home/user/incident_data_source/logs

    Objective:
    1. Create an evidence-handling work area at

        /home/user/incident_response/mirror

       and synchronise BOTH sub-directories (images and logs) from the source directory into that mirror.
       • Perform the two synchronisations in parallel (i.e. start them as background jobs and wait for both to finish).
       • Preserve permissions, ownership and original timestamps during the copy.

    2. After the synchronisation finishes, compute a SHA-256 digest for **every** file that now resides under

        /home/user/incident_response/mirror

    3. Write one consolidated log file at

        /home/user/sync_logs/incident_sync.log

       – the directory /home/user/sync_logs does **not** exist yet; the task requires you to create it with default
         user-only permissions (0700).

    VERY IMPORTANT – log-file format (exactly four pipe-separated fields per line):
        RELATIVE_PATH_FROM_/home/user/incident_response/mirror | FILE_SIZE_BYTES | SHA256_HEX | SYNC_METHOD

        • Use the “|” (pipe) character as the delimiter (no extra spaces).
        • RELATIVE_PATH must not start with “./”, “/”, or any leading spaces.
        • FILE_SIZE_BYTES is the exact byte count **as it sits in the mirror directory** (decimal, no commas).
        • SHA256_HEX must be lowercase hex, 64 characters long.
        • SYNC_METHOD should be the literal word “rsync”.

       The file must contain one line per transferred file, sorted lexicographically
       by the RELATIVE_PATH field.

    Example (illustrative only – your actual numbers and files will differ):

        pictures/photo1.jpg|18345|6e340b9cffb37a989ca544e6bb780a2c49c6e51a4a3696c5b9d1e72e8f5f9fef|rsync

    4. Once the log is written, display its contents to stdout so a human can quickly inspect it.

    Verification checklist (what the automated grader will look for):

    • Directory /home/user/incident_response/mirror exists.
    • It contains two sub-directories, images and logs, each mirroring the source
      with identical file counts, file sizes, permissions and modification times.
    • A log file exists at /home/user/sync_logs/incident_sync.log and is world-readable
      only by your user (0600 or 0640 is acceptable; nothing more permissive).
    • The log file has exactly four non-blank lines, each conforming to the format
      described above.
    • The SHA-256 checksums and file sizes in the log match the actual files in
      /home/user/incident_response/mirror.
    • The RELATIVE_PATH portion of every line matches the path in the mirror
      (e.g. “images/incident_pic1.jpg”, **not** an absolute path).
    • The SYNC_METHOD field for all lines is the literal string “rsync”.
truth:
Initial on-disk state before the agent begins:

    1. Directories
       /home/user/incident_data_source
       ├── images
       └── logs

    2. Files and exact contents

       /home/user/incident_data_source/images/incident_pic1.jpg
       ─── content (ASCII, no newline):          a
       ─── SHA-256: ca978112ca1bbdcafac231b39a23dc4da786eff8147c4e72b9807785afee48bb
       ─── Size: 1 byte

       /home/user/incident_data_source/images/incident_pic2.png
       ─── content (ASCII, no newline):          abc
       ─── SHA-256: ba7816bf8f01cfea414140de5dae2223b00361a396177a9cb410ff61f20015ad
       ─── Size: 3 bytes

       /home/user/incident_data_source/logs/app.log
       ─── content (ASCII, no newline):          hello
       ─── SHA-256: 2cf24dba5fb0a30e26e83b2ac5b9e29e1b161e5c1fa7425e73043362938b9824
       ─── Size: 5 bytes

       /home/user/incident_data_source/logs/error.log
       ─── content (ASCII, no newline):          test
       ─── SHA-256: 9f86d081884c7d659a2feaa0c55ad015a3bf4f1b2b0b822cd15d6c15b0f00a08
       ─── Size: 4 bytes

    Expected final state after successful task completion:

    A. Directory structure copied to

       /home/user/incident_response/mirror
       ├── images
       │   ├── incident_pic1.jpg  (1 byte, identical hash)
       │   └── incident_pic2.png  (3 bytes, identical hash)
       └── logs
           ├── app.log            (5 bytes, identical hash)
           └── error.log          (4 bytes, identical hash)

       All files must keep the same modification timestamps they had in
       /home/user/incident_data_source.

    B. Log file
       /home/user/sync_logs/incident_sync.log
       with exactly the following four lines, **in this order**:

       images/incident_pic1.jpg|1|ca978112ca1bbdcafac231b39a23dc4da786eff8147c4e72b9807785afee48bb|rsync
       images/incident_pic2.png|3|ba7816bf8f01cfea414140de5dae2223b00361a396177a9cb410ff61f20015ad|rsync
       logs/app.log|5|2cf24dba5fb0a30e26e83b2ac5b9e29e1b161e5c1fa7425e73043362938b9824|rsync
       logs/error.log|4|9f86d081884c7d659a2feaa0c55ad015a3bf4f1b2b0b822cd15d6c15b0f00a08|rsync

       (The test harness will check the file contents exactly.)

    C. Permissions
       /home/user/sync_logs            – 0700
       /home/user/sync_logs/incident_sync.log – 0600 (0640 also accepted)

    The grader will further verify that the copy operations ran via the rsync
    executable by checking the SYNC_METHOD field in the log file.
